workflows.name: Open status panel and export state

successCriteria: The package contains its core validator and watcher files.
The self-audit sample app loads without console or runtime errors.
The bundled sample supports exportState and importState.
The status panel becomes visible and shows successful export and import messages.
After each watched validation, the generated fix-prompt.txt is copied to the Windows clipboard unless --no-clipboard is used.
The watcher self-handoffs only for newer validated LaunchCheck update ZIPs.
The watcher does not auto-run arbitrary product ZIPs.
The handoff starts the newer watcher with the same Downloads folder and clipboard settings.
Each validation writes ai-iteration-packet.txt.
The clipboard receives ai-iteration-packet.txt, not only fix-prompt.txt.
The AI iteration packet includes product identity, QA contract summary, findings, runtime evidence, screenshot paths, and captured state.
State export data is captured when exportState/importState are available.
The intentionally broken sample app is excluded from package self-audit scans but remains available as a direct known-bad test.
The validator derives a stable productId from launchcheck.qa.json, product.manifest.json, package.json, or the file name.
Each product receives a per-product continuity file under _VALIDATION_STATE.
If a previous exported state exists, the browser validation attempts to load it before product QA workflows.
The AI iteration packet includes product identity, previous product record, continuity file path, and captured state.
Clipboard copy preserves UTF-8 packet text without mojibake.
AI packet header is ASCII-safe for Windows clipboard paths.
Template HTML files are not treated as live app entry points during normal validation.
ZIP input SHA-256 is captured from the original downloaded file, not the extracted folder.
Reports are rewritten after product-state persistence so the AI packet includes savedProductStateFile.
Self-handoff waits for the newer watcher to write a readiness file before the older watcher exits.
If the newer watcher fails to confirm readiness, the older watcher stays alive.
Self-handoff scripts are written to _VALIDATION_WORK\_HANDOFF for debugging.
New watcher supports --handoff-ready and writes a handoff ready JSON file after watch mode starts.
Each validation creates a progress/regression summary.
The validator compares the current build against the previous product record when available.
Progress grade is BASELINE, IMPROVED, HELD, or REGRESSED.
Status regressions, new errors, and newly failed workflows become explicit progress_regression findings.
Product records persist lastMetrics, lastProgress, lastFindings, and lastFailedWorkflows for future comparisons.
The AI iteration packet includes the progress/regression summary.
Report folder timestamps include milliseconds so rapid validations do not overwrite each other.
Watched validations print a visible progress/regression summary in the console.
Single-file validations print a visible progress/regression summary in the console.
The AI iteration packet repeats progress grade in the product identity section.
The AI iteration packet includes both product continuity file and saved product state file when available.
The watcher banner states that newer validated LaunchCheck ZIPs must confirm alive before the old watcher exits.
The watcher banner explains the auto-run boundary: LaunchCheck updates self-handoff; other products validate until they ship a safe same-product launcher.
A postinstall handoff rescue can start the newer watcher if the handoff process reaches npm install but the normal watcher start is delayed.
The watcher uses a single-instance lock so duplicate watchers do not pile up.
The handoff wait timeout is increased to five minutes for slower install/browser setup paths.
The watcher writes an active-watcher lock under _VALIDATION_WORK.
If a duplicate same-or-newer watcher is already active, the later watcher exits cleanly after writing readiness when needed.
Future self-handoff uses direct Node process spawning instead of PowerShell watcher startup.
Direct handoff copies the already validated extraction root to D:\Work before starting the new watcher.
Direct handoff starts the newer watcher before background dependency installation can block readiness.
preinstall and postinstall rescue both attempt to start the watcher during legacy PowerShell handoff installs.
Direct handoff writes node-handoff and installer logs under _VALIDATION_WORK\_HANDOFF.
The readiness wait for direct handoff is based on the new watcher writing --handoff-ready, not on a visible PowerShell window.
The watcher no longer imports validator.js at process startup.
The watcher can start, acquire the active lock, and write --handoff-ready before runtime dependencies are installed.
Validator loading is lazy and happens only when a candidate file is actually validated.
If validator loading fails because dependencies are missing, the watcher runs npm install and Playwright browser install before retrying validation.
v4 handoff uses the clean-room handoff lab mechanism: copy validated root, spawn newer watcher directly, wait for readiness, then exit old watcher.
v4 logs explicit handoff skip reasons for LaunchCheck-looking ZIPs instead of silently continuing.
v4 writes a heartbeat timestamp into the active watcher lock.
v4 installs dependencies only after the newer watcher has been spawned and can confirm readiness.
v4 watcher startup remains minimal and lazy-loads validator.js only when validation is needed.
v4 should print Direct Node handoff launched when a newer validated LaunchCheck ZIP is eligible for promotion.
Dependency startup warnings are hard-blocked so a partially installed watcher does not silently pass.
The paste-now dinger/notification line appears after the clipboard packet is copied.
The release gate copies validated candidates into _VALIDATED_BUILDS buckets.
The release gate writes a launchcheck receipt JSON beside the copied candidate.
The release gate supports --no-release-copy.
The release gate supports --release-mode copy and --release-mode move.
LaunchCheck update ZIPs are not deleted during move mode because handoff may still need the source ZIP.
The validator JSON report includes report.releaseGate after the watcher release gate runs.
The validator JSON report includes output.releaseGate after the watcher release gate runs.
The HTML report includes a RELEASE GATE panel after the watcher release gate runs.
The AI iteration packet includes a RELEASE GATE section after the watcher release gate runs.
The console output prints release gate bucket, action, copied path, receipt path, and failure reason when present.
The release gate object includes attempted true or false.
The release gate object includes bucket PASS, WARN, FAIL, REGRESSION, UNSAFE, or UNKNOWN.
The release gate object includes action copy, move, safe-copy, disabled, or none.
The release gate object includes copiedPath when a candidate is copied or moved.
The release gate object includes receiptPath when a receipt is written.
The release gate object includes failureReason when a release action fails.
The watcher rewrites reports and the AI packet after releaseGate(filePath, result, options) completes.
The clipboard receives the post-release-gate AI packet, not the pre-release packet.
The v4.17 build validates v4.18 as PASS and writes release gate data into the packet.
The v4.18 canary proves release bucket PASS and receipt path output under _VALIDATED_BUILDS\PASS.

The ready-to-paste notification uses terminal bell plus Windows SystemSounds plus console beep fallback after the clipboard packet is copied.
The console output prints Dinger attempted, terminalBell, windowsSound, consoleBeep, and ok fields after validation.
The validator JSON report includes output.notification and report.notification after the watcher notification runs.
The HTML report includes a READY NOTIFICATION panel after the watcher notification runs.
The AI iteration packet includes a READY NOTIFICATION section after the watcher notification runs.
The clipboard receives the post-notification AI packet, not only the pre-notification packet.
Self-handoff survival is verified after readiness before the old watcher exits.
The old watcher only exits after the new watcher PID is alive, the active lock points to the new PID/version, and the heartbeat advances.
A duplicate watcher readiness file is not accepted as durable handoff confirmation.
Direct handoff uses the validated ZIP folder name under D:\Work, matching the last known-good v4.13 folder behavior.
Direct handoff starts background npm and Playwright installation after watcher readiness, matching the last known-good v4.13 startup separation.
If handoff survival fails, the old watcher remains alive and continues watching Downloads.
Startup self-handoff probe is disabled by default and must not block watcher startup.
Executable self-handoff lifecycle probe runs during LaunchCheck candidate validation, not as a blocking startup task.
The watcher uses a polling scanner plus fs.watch assist so Windows file event misses do not strand post-handoff validation.
Polling seeds existing Downloads files on startup so backlog remains ignored unless --scan-existing is supplied.
The self-handoff probe has a bounded timeout and reports phase details instead of hanging indefinitely.
The promoted watcher must validate a post-handoff smoke ZIP before the self-handoff lifecycle probe passes.
